title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Compiler Case: Event Loop Work Queues
summary: The service reads configuration during requests, starts background promises without ownership, mixes module systems, treats signals as ordinary exceptions, and reports ready before dependencies exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts event loop work queues increment in process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Prove a clean npm ci, type gate, test, build, artifact, and startup under a changed environment without warm local residue.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Event Loop Work Queues · Theory Event Loop Work Queues
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5551 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
